items.title: Three young footy stars try to use a cassette player - and the results will dismay any Aussie older than 40

items.title: Missing American college student found dead in Barcelona
items.deck: The body of missing University of Alabama student James Gracey, who disappeared on a trip to Barcelona, has been found, Spanish police said.

items.title: US Mint gets green light to put Trump on 250th gold coin
items.deck: The Commission of Fine Arts (CFA) on Thursday greenlighted a $1 gold coin featuring President Trump’s likeness to commemorate America’s 250th anniversary.

items.title: Calls grow to fund TSA as travelers' frustration grows
items.deck: Fliers were greeted with another day of long lines as TSA officers continue to work without receiving their pay.

items.title: U.N. nuclear watchdog chief on what's next for Iran's nuclear ambitions
items.deck: The head of the United Nations nuclear watchdog agency said that "a lot has survived" of Iran's nuclear capabilities, and military operations alone cannot destroy them.

items.title: Stock markets rattled and energy prices soar after strikes on Qatar gas hub
items.deck: Oil and gas prices have risen again after fresh strikes hit key infrastructure in the Middle East.

items.title: Why Archie Gray is Tottenham's best survival hope: The personality trait that separates him from his team-mates, how he dovetails with Xavi Simons, the unseen moment that proves his character and heavyweight clubs keen to sign him
items.deck: Gray has emerged as a hero of these troubled times for Tottenham and his performance against Atletico Madrid was further evidence he has what it takes to perform at the very top.

items.title: Jerry O’Connell sums up the Dem dilemma perfectly — even if he did have to get beaten up for it
items.deck: I never took Jerry O'Connell for a political guy, but while on Bill Maher's "Club Random Podcast" this week, he inadvertently summed up the Democratic party's current dilemma of being lost in the wilderness.

items.title: Elizabeth Warren jumps into Democratic primary battles across the country
items.deck: Sen. Elizabeth Warren took sides in two competitive Senate primaries on Thursday, part of a broadening play to reshape her party in a handful of important races — and push back on party leaders in one of the most critical battleground campaigns of 2026

items.title: Father of service member killed in Iran war said he never told Pete Hegseth to 'finish' the job
items.deck: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and President Donald Trump have said the families of service members who died in the Iran war told them to finish the job.

items.title: Trump downplays Iran war's impact on global energy prices
items.deck: Hosting the prime minister of Japan in the Oval Office, President Trump downplayed the Iran war's impact on global energy prices.

items.title: US Mint can produce commemorative gold Trump coin following arts panel approval
items.deck: The U.S. Mint can move forward with producing a commemorative gold coin honoring President Donald Trump after a federal arts advisory panel signed off on the design, advancing a controversial proposal tied to the nation’s 250th celebrations. The Commission of Fine Arts, which reviews the design of federal coins, medals, and monuments, approved the concept, […]

items.title: Minnesota senior lobbies for happy hour at assisted living centers
items.deck: HF4145 would allow nursing homes, boarding care homes and assisted-living complexes to display and serve alcohol to residents.

items.title: Iran strikes Qatari natural gas plant after Israeli attack on key Iranian gas field
items.deck: In a dramatic escalation, Iran launched a strike on the world's largest liquified natural gas plant in Qatar and oil refineries in Saudi Arabia and Kuwait.

items.title: Useless European ‘allies’: Letters to the Editor — March 20, 2026

items.title: Former Man United and Chelsea goalkeeper coach dies aged 67: Tributes pour in for Jose Mourinho's close aide who was tasked with coaching David de Gea and Petr Cech
items.deck: A former member of Jose Mourinho's long-term backroom staff member has died aged 67.

items.title: Minnesota Audit: State Agency 'Accidentally' Blocked Kickback Investigation Into Autism Services
items.deck: Minnesota Audit: State Agency 'Accidentally' Blocked Kickback Investigation Into Autism Services Authored by Janice Hisle via The Epoch Times (emphasis ours), A state agency erred when it blocked autism-services kickbacks from being investigated—a decision based on the agency’s flawed, decades-old definition of “fraud,” according to a Minnesota audit released March 17.

items.title: Nobody owns Donald Trump — including his Israel-hating ex-allies

items.title: Over 170 million units of disinfecting pads recalled because they may not be sterile
items.deck: The FDA announced that 170 million units of the two-ply Webcol Alcohol Prep pad are being recalled.
